insights:
The single best deal among the current crop is inclusionAI's Ling-2.6-flash, which tops the composite value score at an extraordinary 840.0. It achieves this through a combination of a very low implied cost and a competitive 180 tokens per second, with a generous 262K context window. However, its intelligence score is only 14.0 out of 60, meaning it is suitable primarily for simple classification, extraction, or low-stakes text generation where high accuracy is not required. For developers who can tolerate a less capable model, Ling-2.6-flash offers unmatched throughput per dollar.

Two notable runners-up provide a better intelligence-to-cost trade-off. DeepSeek V4 Flash scores 40.0 in intelligence and a value score of 319.8, delivering 127 tokens per second and a massive 1000K context window, making it a strong all-rounder for more demanding tasks. MiMo-V2.5, with 37.0 intelligence and a value score of 198.2, is slower at 63 tokens per second but excels in coding with a Code ELO of 1427 and also supports 1000K context, ideal for code generation and repair. Tencent's Hy3 leads all models in raw intelligence at 41.0, yet its value score of 143.0 and 59 tokens per second make it less economical for cost-sensitive workloads, though it remains a solid choice if intelligence is the top priority.

For the most common cost-sensitive API use case, where you need reliable performance without breaking the budget, DeepSeek V4 Flash is the actionable recommendation. It delivers high intelligence (40/60) at a value score nearly double that of the next best comparable model, with ample speed and context length. For purely trivial tasks, Ling-2.6-flash remains unbeatable on cost, but for any production application requiring genuine reasoning or factual accuracy, DeepSeek V4 Flash is the optimal balance between capability and expense.
